g.text: Null now at bit 5 (position from str start)

div.p: Key Insight: The aligned load at 0x1000 is always safe because:

ol.li: 16-byte aligned reads cannot cross a 4KB page boundary
The aligned address is in the same page as the original pointer Masking Logic:

ul.li: We ignore them by masking or shifting
The null at byte 8 is found correctly
Position 5 is returned (length of "hello") Page Safety Proof:
